opp.name: Demiroren S2P
opp.next_step: April 2026: Met at Horizon. Very keen in exploring Zycus solutions and have asked for an introductory meeting.
opp.ais_why:
• The SF Description captures the Horizon-event-driven engagement: 'This is the Horizon lead opp - Met with Zycus team in Horizon 2026. 7/04/26 KAYA GULSEN Purchasing Manager had a call with him, he mentioned they are highly interested in Zycus products, they have not replied to the emails because there calendars are completely blocked, they will revert back to us with the schedule for demo.' This is generic 'highly interested in Zycus products' language with no AI/GenAI/agentic framing ‚Äî broad product interest, not AI-specific.
• Merlin and Merlin Intake in product scope plus ML iAnalyze; no ANA, no Agentic AI in product list. BD_Engagement is Unengaged. Zero Avoma meetings on file. KAYA GULSEN is the named buyer-side contact (Purchasing Manager) but the engagement is at pre-demo scheduling stage with calendar-availability friction. There's no captured AI dialogue from KAYA or any other Demiroren stakeholder.
• Medium confidence: clear Description text indicates traditional Horizon-lead-driven discovery motion. Defaulted DOWN at 3/4 boundary given no AI champion or POC evidence. The Description's framing of 'highly interested in Zycus products' is broad-product interest rather than AI-led interest; would re-evaluate after the first demo confirms scope and AI relevance.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (Horizon meeting Apr 2026, intro request)
Avoma discovery: zero buyer calls found
SF Business_Objectives__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c: not populated
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case on record. The Next_Step__c field reports the buyer met Zycus at Horizon in April 2026 and expressed interest in 'exploring Zycus solutions', but no discovery call transcript, Business_Objectives__c entry, or Customer_Business_Problem__c detail exists to capture the pain, cost impact, headcount savings, cycle-time reduction, or error-rate improvement they are trying to solve. The product scope (full S2P suite plus Merlin AI) implies a broad transformation but the underlying business case is unconfirmed. Without a discovery call to document their current-state pain and desired outcomes, the metrics element is a genuine knowledge gap. The rank-1 recommended move is to conduct the requested introductory/discovery call and use it to quantify the procurement inefficiency, manual workload, or compliance risk driving this evaluation.

sources: SF Contact Roles: no Champion role assigned
SF Champion_Identified__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c: not populated
Avoma: zero calls, no advocacy evidence
Next_Step__c: intro meeting requested (interest, not championship)
champion.narrative: Champion is not identified. The Contact Role roster includes four buyer-side stakeholders (Semih Dogan DM, KAYA GULSEN + Gokha Dinc Evaluators, Fatih Gök Influencer) but none is marked Champion, and zero buyer calls means no evidence of advocacy (introductions, shared internal docs, pushing back on competitors for us, driving the agenda). The Next_Step__c field says the buyer 'asked for an introductory meeting' after Horizon (Apr 2026), which is a positive signal of interest but not active championship. Champion_Identified__c and X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c are not populated. The gap is genuine: we have engaged contacts but no confirmed champion. The recommended move is to use the introductory/discovery call and subsequent demos to assess which stakeholder(s) show the strongest advocacy: who volunteers internal intelligence (budget, competitors, decision timeline), who introduces us to the EB/CFO, who defends Zycus when objections arise. Then develop that person into an active champion by giving them a win (a proof point they can take to leadership, a quick-hit ROI model, early access to Merlin). Document champion strength and re-assess every 30 days; a weak or cooling champion in a complex enterprise deal is a leading indicator of stall or loss.

sources: SF Competitors__c: not populated
SF competition synthesis fields: not populated or not present
Avoma: zero calls, no competitor mentions
Next_Step__c: intro meeting requested, competitive context unknown
competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is not populated, and the synthesis-map free-text fields (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c) that often encode competitive intel are also empty or not present in this org. Zero buyer calls means no verbal mention of other vendors being evaluated (e.g. 'we are also looking at Coupa/Ariba/GEP/Ivalua'). The Next_Step__c field says the buyer met Zycus at Horizon and requested an intro meeting, which could mean Zycus is early in their consideration set or could mean they are already running a competitive RFI/RFP and we are joining mid-stream—both are possible, both are unconfirmed. The product scope (full S2P + Merlin AI) and the enterprise scale (Demiroren Holding is a large Turkish conglomerate) suggest the buyer will evaluate multiple vendors, likely including SAP Ariba (dominant in large EU/MEA enterprises), Coupa (cloud SaaS leader), GEP (strong in S2P), Ivalua (procurement suite), and possibly Jaggaer or Zycus's other typical competitors. But that is inference, not evidence. The gap is genuine: we do not know if we are sole-sourced, in a shortlist, or competing in a wide RFI. The recommended move is to ask directly on the discovery call: are you evaluating other vendors, who are they, and where are you in that process. Document the competitive landscape and the buyer's sentiment toward each rival, then tailor the Zycus positioning (Merlin AI differentiation, Turkish localization, mid-market agility vs Ariba/Coupa complexity) to wedge against the known threats.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c: not populated
Avoma: zero calls, no pain discussion
Next_Step__c: 'very keen' signals interest, pain unconfirmed
identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational or financial pain is not documented. The Next_Step__c field reports the buyer met Zycus at Horizon (Apr 2026) and is 'very keen in exploring Zycus solutions', which signals interest but not the underlying pain. No discovery call transcript, Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, or Compelling_Event__c entry exists to capture what is broken today (manual procurement, slow cycle times, poor spend visibility, contract risk, supplier fragmentation, invoice errors) or what the cost of inaction is (What_if_this_is_not_done__c is not populated). The product scope (full S2P + Merlin AI) implies the buyer wants to modernize and automate procurement end-to-end, but that is inference, not confirmed pain. The pain owner is also unknown (likely Semih Dogan, Group Director Procurement, given his DM role, but unconfirmed). The gap is genuine: we know they are interested but not why or how urgently. The recommended move is to use the introductory/discovery call to ask: what is the specific problem you are trying to solve, what is the impact today (cost, time, risk), what happens if you do nothing, and who owns this pain internally. Quote their answers verbatim and tie the pain to a compelling event (budget cycle, contract expiration, growth/M&A, regulatory pressure) if one exists.

sources: SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c: not populated
Avoma: zero calls, no contracting/legal discussion
Stage: Qualified (paper process not yet engaged)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, SI/partner role, implementation scoping) is unknown. No call, task, or Salesforce field captures whether Demiroren runs vendor contracts through a central procurement team, requires InfoSec/GDPR review (likely given EU/Turkey geography and enterprise scale), uses a partner/SI for implementation, or has a standard MSA/SOW template. The Current_Contract_Expiration__c field (a forcing function for incumbent replacement) is not populated, so no contract-expiration deadline is driving urgency. The deal is in Qualified stage (discovery/early eval), so paper-process detail is not yet expected, but the gap will block late-stage forecasting if not filled before Vendor Selected. The recommended move is to ask on the discovery call or during commercials: what is your standard contracting process, does legal/InfoSec need to review before signature, and do you work with a partner for ERP integration and implementation. Document the answer and flag any InfoSec or legal timeline that could delay close.

sources: SF Contact Roles: Semih Dogan (DM), Fatih Gök (Influencer)
SF X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c: not populated
Avoma: zero calls, no verbal EB confirmation
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified. The Contact Role roster includes Semih Dogan (Group Director, Procurement) marked as Decision Maker, plus two Evaluators (KAYA GULSEN, Gokha Dinc, both Purchasing Managers) and one Influencer (Fatih Gök, Finance Controller). Semih Dogan's title (Group Director) suggests operational authority over procurement, but no evidence exists that he controls the commercial/pricing conversation or has final budget sign-off for a $200k enterprise SaaS deal spanning the full S2P stack plus AI. Fatih Gök (Finance Controller) may be closer to budget authority, but his role is marked Influencer, not EB, and no call or field captures his involvement in pricing or CFO-level sponsorship. The synthesis-map fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) are not populated, and zero buyer calls means no verbal confirmation of who holds commercial authority. The gap is genuine: we have an operational Decision Maker but no confirmed Economic Buyer. The recommended move is to ask Semih Dogan on the discovery call who will approve the budget and whether CFO or C-level sign-off is required, then map that person into the stakeholder roster.

sources: SF What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c: not populated
Avoma: zero calls, no process discussion
Contact Roles show procurement + finance, approval chain unconfirmed
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is not documented. The call sequence itself (section-2 rule: 'who appears at which stage, who joins late, who escalates') cannot be read because zero buyer calls exist. The synthesis-map fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) are not populated. The stakeholder roster shows a Group Director (Semih Dogan, DM) plus two Purchasing Manager Evaluators and a Finance Controller Influencer, which suggests a procurement-led process with finance review, but the approval chain (single-level vs multi-level, CFO sign-off required, board approval for $200k) is unknown. The Next_Step__c field says the buyer requested an intro meeting after Horizon (Apr 2026); that meeting has not yet occurred, so the process has not been discussed. The gap is genuine. The recommended move is to ask on the discovery call: what is your vendor selection process (RFI, RFP, shortlist, demos, workshops, pricing, legal), who needs to approve, and what is the timeline for each gate. Map the answer into the record and use it to forecast stage progression realistically.

sources: SF Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c: not populated
Avoma: zero calls, no criteria discussion
Product scope (8 modules) implies complex eval, criteria inferred but unconfirmed
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are not documented. No discovery call transcript, RFI/RFP content, Business_Requirements__c entry, or Top_Challenges_Priorities__c detail exists to capture what Demiroren will evaluate vendors against. The product scope (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) is comprehensive—covering sourcing, contracting, supplier management, procure-to-pay, invoicing, analytics, and AI intake—so the evaluation will almost certainly involve functional fit, AI/Merlin capability, ERP integration (given the enterprise scale), user adoption/change management, and price/ROI. But those are inferences, not confirmed criteria. The Next_Step__c field says the buyer 'asked for an introductory meeting' after Horizon; that meeting has not yet happened (no call recorded), so the criteria remain unknown. The recommended move is to use the introductory/discovery call to ask directly: what are the top 3-5 decision criteria, what is non-negotiable (e.g. AI capability, SAP integration, Turkish localization), and how will vendors be scored. Document the answer and reconcile it with any forthcoming RFI/RFP.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c on record, and zero buyer calls means no verbal appetite for AI captured. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake (agentic procurement AI), and the Next_Step__c field says the buyer is 'very keen in exploring Zycus solutions' after Horizon Apr 2026—Horizon typically features Merlin, so the buyer may have seen the AI story and that may be driving interest—but that is inference, not confirmed AI hunger. Tier: unknown until discovery call validates appetite. The recommended move is to probe AI appetite on the discovery call: ask what manual, repetitive procurement work they want to eliminate, and position Merlin as the automation layer. Their response (enthusiasm vs skepticism) will tier them as AI Hungry, AI Curious, or AI Resistant.

items.expected_effect: Converts stalled introduction into qualified pipeline; unblocks demo/RFI progression; maps MEDDPICC (pain, metrics, EB, criteria, process, competition) required to advance and forecast realistically. Without this call the deal remains dark and cannot move forward.

Bring peer ROI proof point (e.g. 'X company cut sourcing cycle time 40% with Merlin intake-to-award automation') to anchor the value story.

confidence_signals.summary: Weak confidence signals. Positive: buyer requested intro meeting post-Horizon Apr 2026 ('very keen', Next_Step__c), product scope is comprehensive (8 modules including Merlin AI), Decision Maker (Semih Dogan) mapped, $200k amount and 266-day close date are credible for enterprise S2P. Negative: zero buyer calls (no discovery conducted 71 days post-Horizon introduction), 29-day silence since last activity 28 May, no champion, no EB, no pain/metrics/criteria/competition documented, and no MEDDPICC coverage. Cooling risk: moderate (intro meeting not scheduled/conducted suggests deprioritization or internal delays). Overall: early-stage deal with genuine buyer interest but stalled execution; confidence is low until discovery call happens and validates the opportunity.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 19 Mar 2026, 99 days old. Qualified stage reached same day (no submission-date series). 266 days to close date 19 Mar 2027. LastActivityDate 28 May 2026 (29 days ago). Next_Step__c reports Horizon meeting Apr 2026 and request for introductory meeting; no subsequent call logged, no demo scheduled, and no buyer reply captured since then. Forward pace required: discovery call, demo, RFI/RFP cycle, shortlist, ShoeFit/BRD, workshop, commercials, negotiation, contracting in 266 days is feasible IF engagement restarts this week, but current 29-day silence signals stalling post-introduction.

north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified-stage deal with 266 days to close has stalled 29 days since last activity; buyer requested intro meeting post-Horizon but no discovery call logged, no champion identified, and no engagement restart despite four-contact lineup including Decision Maker.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal (Qualified, 99 days old) stalled 29 days post-Horizon introduction with no discovery call conducted. Three levers to restart momentum and raise win probability: (1) conduct the requested discovery call this week to map pain/metrics/criteria/EB and convert interest into qualified pipeline; (2) multi-thread beyond the four known contacts (procurement + finance) to reach C-level sponsorship (CFO or COO for a $200k enterprise S2P+AI transformation); (3) position Merlin AI as the differentiator in the discovery narrative (agentic procurement, intake-to-contract automation, AI-driven sourcing) to create urgency and wedge against likely Ariba/Coupa competition.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown. No competitor named in Salesforce fields (Competitors__c not populated), zero buyer calls to surface verbal mentions, and no Next_Step__c / task intel. The product scope (full S2P + Merlin AI) and enterprise scale suggest the buyer will evaluate multiple vendors—likely SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua—but that is inference, not evidence. The gap blocks wedge development and win-strategy planning. The recommended move is to ask directly on the discovery call: are you evaluating other vendors, who, and where are you in that process. No current threat can be ranked because no competitor is confirmed.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is unvalidated. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake (agentic AI for procurement), which is Zycus's strongest 2026 differentiation, but zero discovery calls means the AI appetite and the Merlin value prop have not been discussed with the buyer. The Next_Step__c field says the buyer is 'very keen in exploring Zycus solutions' post-Horizon Apr 2026, and Horizon typically features Merlin/AI prominently, so the buyer may have seen the AI narrative and that may be driving their interest—but that is inference, not confirmed. No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c on record to indicate AI hunger. The recommended move is to lead the discovery call with AI: ask what manual procurement processes they want to automate (intake, sourcing, contracting, invoice exceptions), position Merlin as the agentic layer that eliminates that work, and bring a proof point (peer ROI story). If the buyer is AI-hungry, Merlin becomes the wedge; if AI-curious or resistant, adapt but do not drop the narrative. Current score: unvalidated, no evidence of under-positioning because no positioning conversation has occurred yet.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is unconfirmed. The product scope (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) is comprehensive and signals Zycus can cover the buyer's full S2P need, but zero discovery calls means no criteria, no pain points, and no evaluation framework to benchmark fit against. The Next_Step__c field says the buyer is 'very keen in exploring Zycus solutions' (positive sentiment, Horizon Apr 2026), but keen interest is not validated fit. The recommended move is to use the discovery call to map their requirements (functional, AI, integration, localization) against Zycus capability and document where we align, where we partially align, and where we are exposed (e.g. if they require deep SAP/Oracle ERP integration or Turkish regulatory compliance features Zycus does not natively support).

This is generic 'highly interested in Zycus products' language with no AI/GenAI/agentic framing ‚Äî broad product interest, not AI-specific.
• Merlin and Merlin Intake in product scope plus ML iAnalyze; no ANA, no Agentic AI in product list. BD_Engagement is Unengaged. Zero Avoma meetings on file. KAYA GULSEN is the named buyer-side contact (Purchasing Manager) but the engagement is at pre-demo scheduling stage with calendar-availability friction. There's no captured AI dialogue from KAYA or any other Demiroren stakeholder.
• Medium confidence: clear Description text indicates traditional Horizon-lead-driven discovery motion. Defaulted DOWN at 3/4 boundary given no AI champion or POC evidence. The Description's framing of 'highly interested in Zycus products' is broad-product interest rather than AI-led interest; would re-evaluate after the first demo confirms scope and AI relevance.

deltas.subject: Single-thread risk in a $200k enterprise S2P+AI deal. Four contacts on record (Semih Dogan DM, KAYA GULSEN + Gokha Dinc Evaluators, Fatih Gök Influencer), all procurement/finance operational roles; no C-level sponsor (CFO, COO, CPO) mapped. For a deal spanning 8 modules (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) and $200k, budget sign-off will require executive sponsorship. The recommended move is to ask Semih Dogan on the discovery call: who will approve the budget for this investment, and can you introduce us to that person (likely CFO or COO). Then multi-thread up to secure executive sponsorship before commercials. A procurement-only thread in a large enterprise deal is a ghost/stall risk if the exec layer never engages.

deltas.subject: Discovery call not conducted 29 days after buyer requested intro meeting (28 May last activity, no call logged). The deal is stalled at the gate: without a discovery call to capture pain, metrics, criteria, EB, process, and competition, the deal cannot advance to demo/RFI and the 266-day runway to close is burning down. The highest-leverage move is to schedule and conduct the discovery call this week (by 03 Jul 2026) with Semih Dogan (DM) and ideally Fatih Gök (Finance Controller, potential EB). Use the call to document the business problem (procurement inefficiency, spend visibility, contract risk), quantify the impact (cost, time, headcount), map the decision criteria and process (RFI/RFP/shortlist/demo timeline, approval chain), identify the Economic Buyer (CFO or C-level), and surface the competitive landscape (are they evaluating Ariba/Coupa/GEP/Ivalua, where are we in the consideration set). This single call converts a stalled introduction into a qualified, actionable deal.

deltas.subject: Single-thread risk in a $200k enterprise S2P+AI deal. Four contacts on record (Semih Dogan DM, KAYA GULSEN + Gokha Dinc Evaluators, Fatih Gök Influencer), all procurement/finance operational roles; no C-level sponsor (CFO, COO, CPO) mapped. For a deal spanning 8 modules (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) and $200k, budget sign-off will require executive sponsorship. The recommended move is to ask Semih Dogan on the discovery call: who will approve the budget for this investment, and can you introduce us to that person (likely CFO or COO). Then multi-thread up to secure executive sponsorship before commercials. A procurement-only thread in a large enterprise deal is a ghost/stall risk if the exec layer never engages.

deltas.to: Merlin AI positioning opportunity in a full-suite S2P deal. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake (agentic AI for procurement), which is Zycus's s
deltas.subject: Merlin AI positioning opportunity in a full-suite S2P deal. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake (agentic AI for procurement), which is Zycus's strongest differentiation vs Ariba/Coupa/GEP in 2026. But zero discovery calls means the AI appetite and the Merlin value prop have not yet been validated with the buyer. The recommended move is to lead the discovery call with AI: ask what manual, high-touch procurement processes they want to automate (intake, sourcing, contract redlining, supplier onboarding, invoice exceptions), then position Merlin as the agentic layer that eliminates that manual work. Bring a proof point (a peer customer ROI story: 'X company cut sourcing cycle time 40% with Merlin-powered intake-to-award automation') and tie it to their pain. If the buyer is AI-hungry (likely given 2026 enterprise procurement trends), Merlin becomes the wedge that moves Zycus from 'another S2P vendor' to 'the AI-native platform'. If they are AI-curious or resistant, adapt the narrative but do not drop it—Merlin is the competitive moat.
